title: Confirmation of Conference
act: Family Law Rules, O. Reg. 114/99, Rule 17(14)

urgencyFlag.deadlineRule: 2:00 PM, 3 business days before conference date (weekends and statutory holidays excluded)

messages.critical: ⚠️ URGENT — Your conference confirmation is due TODAY by 2:00 PM. File immediately at the courthouse or through Justice Services Online. If missed, your conference will be cancelled.
messages.warning: Your conference is coming up. This confirmation must be filed by 2:00 PM, 3 business days before the conference date. File it now to avoid cancellation.
messages.reminder: Reminder: You need to file this confirmation at least 3 business days before your conference. Give the other party a copy first.

urgencyFlag.cancellationWarning: Under Rule 17(14.1), the court SHALL cancel your conference if this form is not filed on time — unless the court orders otherwise. There is no grace period.

bannerThresholds.note: Thresholds are hours remaining until the CALCULATED deadline (not until conference). FormEngine must compute deadline = conferenceDate - 3 business days at 14:00, then compare datetime.now() to deadline.

critical.message: ⚠️ URGENT — Your filing deadline is TODAY by 2:00 PM. File immediately or your conference will be automatically cancelled.

warning.message: Your filing deadline is {deadline_date} at 2:00 PM. File soon — missing this deadline cancels your conference automatically.

info.message: Reminder: File by 2:00 PM on {deadline_date} — 3 business days before your conference.

ok.message: Your filing deadline is {deadline_date}. You have time — don't forget.

urgencyFlag.deadlineNote: Rule 17(14)(c) says 'three days' — courts consistently interpret this as three business days (excluding weekends and Ontario statutory holidays), confirmed by the Ontario Courts website, Steps to Justice, and all regional Practice Directions.

description: Filed to confirm that you will attend an upcoming case conference, settlement conference, or trial management conference. Must be filed by 2:00 PM at least 3 business days before the conference date. Give the other party a copy BEFORE filing with the court. If you miss this deadline, your conference will be cancelled automatically.

steps.title: Court & File Information
steps.description: Enter the court location, file number, and conference details.

fields.label: Name of Court
fields.hint: Select the courthouse where your conference is scheduled.
options: Ontario Court of Justice — North Bay
Ontario Court of Justice — Owen Sound
Ontario Court of Justice — Sault Ste. Marie
Ontario Court of Justice — St. Catharines
Ontario Court of Justice — St. Thomas
Ontario Court of Justice — Thunder Bay
Superior Court of Justice — North Bay
Superior Court of Justice — St. Catharines
Superior Court of Justice — Thunder Bay
Superior Court of Justice — Toronto (393 University Ave.)
Superior Court of Justice — Toronto (47 Sheppard Ave.)

fields.label: Court Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 393 University Avenue, Toronto, ON M5G 1E6

validation.message: Enter the file number exactly as it appears on your court documents.

steps.title: Applicant & Respondent
steps.description: Enter the full legal names and contact details for both parties.

fields.label: Applicant — Address for Service (street, city, postal code)
fields.placeholder: e.g. 123 Main Street, Toronto, ON M1A 1A1

validation.message: Enter a valid telephone number including area code.

fields.label: Applicant's Lawyer (name, address, phone) — if applicable
fields.placeholder:
e.g. A. Jones, 456 Bay Street, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, 416-555-0200
Leave blank if self-represented.

fields.label: Respondent — Address for Service (street, city, postal code)
fields.placeholder: e.g. 789 Queen Street, Toronto, ON M5H 2N2

fields.label: Respondent's Lawyer (name, address, phone) — if applicable
fields.placeholder: Leave blank if respondent is self-represented or if unknown.

steps.title: Who Is Filing + Conferring
steps.description: Identify yourself and confirm whether you conferred with the other party as required.

fields.label: Filing Requirement — Conferring
fields.content:
The Family Law Rules (Rule 17(3.1)) require you to discuss the following with the other party (or their lawyer) before filing this form:

a) Requests for financial disclosure
b) A temporary resolution of the outstanding issues in dispute
c) For a settlement conference or trial management conference: a final resolution of the issues in dispute

This can be done by phone, email, or in writing.

Exceptions — you do NOT have to confer if:
• A court order prohibits you from communicating with the other party, OR
• There is a risk of domestic violence and the other party does not have a lawyer

(Rule 17(3.2))

fields.label: Have you conferred (or made best efforts to confer) with the other party about: (a) financial disclosure requests, (b) temporary resolution of issues, and (c) for settlement/TMC: final resolution of issues?

options.label: Yes — we have conferred (or attempted to confer) on all required matters
No — I was unable to confer (explain below)

fields.label: Reason for not conferring:
fields.placeholder:
e.g. A restraining order prohibits direct communication between the parties.
e.g. There is a risk of domestic violence and the other party does not have a lawyer (Rule 17(3.2)(b)).
e.g. I attempted to contact the respondent by email on [date] and by phone on [date] but received no response.
fields.hint: Be specific. The court may make a cost order against a party who fails to confer without good reason.

fields.label: I confirm that the parties have discussed costs.
fields.hint: Field 9 on Form 17F requires confirmation that costs have been discussed. This is mandatory.

steps.title: Conference Date & Type
steps.description: Enter the date, time, and type of conference. This triggers the deadline urgency check.

fields.label: This is a:
fields.hint: Select the type of conference exactly as it appears in your court notice or order.

fields.label: Scheduled Date of Conference
fields.hint: Enter the date your conference is scheduled. We will automatically calculate your filing deadline (2:00 PM, 3 business days before this date) and alert you if you are running short on time.

validation.message: Conference date must be in the future.

fields.label: Scheduled Time of Conference
fields.hint: Enter the time the conference is scheduled to begin.

fields.label: Case Management Judge (if any)
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Honourable Justice A. Lee
fields.hint: If a case management judge is assigned to your case, enter their name. Leave blank if none.

fields.label: Your Filing Deadline
fields.displayFormat: Your confirmation must be filed by 2:00 PM on {deadline_date}.

steps.title: Issues & Conference Brief
steps.description: List the issues for the conference and identify the documents the judge should read.

fields.label: Issues to be discussed at this conference (list each one separately):
fields.placeholder:
e.g.
a) Decision-making responsibility for Emma (age 6) and Liam (age 4)
b) Parenting time schedule — week-on, week-off proposal
c) Child support — table amount under Federal Child Support Guidelines
d) Equalization of net family property
e) Disclosure of financial documents
fields.hint: Be specific. List each issue separately with a letter (a, b, c...). Courts may refuse to discuss issues not listed here.

validation.message: You must list at least one issue for the conference.

fields.label: Have you filed your Conference Brief (Form 17A for case conference / Form 17C for settlement conference / Form 17E for trial management conference)?

options.label: No — I am filing the brief at the same time as this confirmation
No brief required for this type of conference

fields.hint:
Conference briefs must be served and filed:
• At least 6 days before (if you requested the conference, or are the applicant)
• At least 4 days before (if the other party requested it, or you are the respondent)

Note for Trial Management Conference in Superior Court of Justice: The required documents may not be Form 17E. Instead, the court may require a trial scheduling endorsement form, an offer to settle, and an opening statement outline. Check with the court office before filing. Form 17E applies to OCJ trial management conferences.

fields.label: In addition to the conference brief, the presiding judge will be referred to the following pages/tabs (list with volume, tab, and page numbers):
fields.placeholder:
e.g.
a) Financial Statement of Jane Smith (Form 13) — Volume 2, Tab C, page 45
b) Affidavit of Jane Smith sworn June 10, 2025 — Volume 1, Tab B, page 12
c) Applicant's Offer to Settle dated May 1, 2025 — Volume 3, Tab F
fields.hint: Use exact volume, tab, and page numbers from the Continuing Record. This helps the judge navigate your materials quickly.

steps.title: Time Estimates
steps.description: Estimate how much time each party needs at the conference.

fields.label: Time estimate — Applicant (minutes):
fields.hint: How many minutes does the applicant (or their lawyer) need to present their position at the conference?

validation.message: Enter a time between 5 and 480 minutes.

fields.label: Time estimate — Respondent (minutes):
fields.hint: How many minutes does the respondent (or their lawyer) need?

fields.label: Total time estimate (auto-calculated)
fields.hint: This is the combined estimate that will appear on the form. Confirm it is realistic — courts cancel conferences that run over time.

fields.label: Any note about time estimates (optional):
fields.placeholder: e.g. Both parties are self-represented and may need additional guidance from the judge.

steps.title: Delivery, Deadline & Signature
steps.description: Confirm delivery to the other party, acknowledge the deadline, and sign.

fields.label: Deadline Reminder
fields.content:
⏰ FILING DEADLINE: You must file this Confirmation with the court by 2:00 PM, at least 3 business days before your conference date.

Business days exclude weekends and Ontario statutory holidays.

Examples:
• Conference on Monday → file by 2:00 PM the previous Wednesday
• Conference on Thursday → file by 2:00 PM Monday of the same week
• Conference on Friday → file by 2:00 PM Tuesday of the same week

You can file by:
1. In person at the courthouse
2. By email to the court office
3. Through Justice Services Online (justice.gov.on.ca)
4. Through the Ontario Courts Public Portal (ontariocourts.ca/apply) — whichever is available at your courthouse

Note: Fax is no longer accepted for this form.

(Rule 17(14)(c) as amended by O.Reg. 228/25)

fields.label: I confirm that I have given (or will give before filing) a copy of this Confirmation to the other party or their lawyer.
fields.hint:
Under Rule 17(14)(b), you must deliver a copy to the other party before filing with the clerk. You do NOT need to file a Form 6B (Affidavit of Service) for this step.

Exception: If your case is a child protection case under the Child, Youth and Family Services Act, 2017, you do NOT need to give the other party a copy before filing.

fields.label: I confirm that all information in this Confirmation of Conference is accurate.

I understand that if any information in this form becomes incorrect before the conference is held, I must immediately update the form and deliver the corrected version to the other party and the court clerk (as required by Rule 17(14.1.1)).
fields.hint: This is a legal obligation under the Family Law Rules. If circumstances change — for example, a settlement conference settles some issues — you must file a corrected Form 17F right away.

fields.label: Hearth & Page Notice
fields.content: This form was prepared using Hearth & Page (hearthandpage.ca). File it with the court by 2:00 PM, 3 business days before your conference date. Deliver a copy to the other party first. Hearth & Page is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. This document was prepared on or by Hearth & Page.

sections.label: 1. My name is, and I am the
2. I have conferred (or attempted to confer) with the other party on: (a) outstanding issues; (b) resolution; (c) conference materials; (d) time estimates
3. The scheduled conference type and date
7. Documents for the judge (in addition to the conference brief)
10. I will update this form if the information changes

pdfTemplate.footerText: FLR 17F (September 24, 2024) — Prepared using Hearth & Page (hearthandpage.ca). This document was prepared on or by Hearth & Page.

validationRules.message: You must list at least one issue for the conference.
Time estimates must be at least 5 minutes each.
You must confirm that you have given the other party a copy of this form before filing.
You must explain why you were unable to confer with the other party.
You must confirm that the parties have discussed costs (Field 9 on Form 17F — required by Rule 17).
You must confirm that all information in this Confirmation is accurate.
You must acknowledge your obligation to update this form if any information changes before the conference (Rule 17(14.1.1)).
